introduction:
In West Bengal, the Caste Certificate is issued by the Block Development Officer (BDO) / Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) for SC and ST applicants. For OBC (OBC-A and OBC-B), the certificate is issued by the Backward Classes Welfare Department.

Applications can be made online via the e-District West Bengal Portal or offline at the BDO / SDO office. SC/ST certificates are issued under the Revenue Department while OBC requires additional income verification.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • SC/ST: Issued by BDO / SDO; OBC: by Backward Classes Welfare Department
  • Processing time: 15–30 working days
  • Fee: ₹0 (Free) for SC/ST; nominal for OBC
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC-A / OBC-B renewed periodically
  • Online portal: e-District West Bengal (edistrict.wb.gov.in)
  • OBC-A includes Muslim OBC communities; OBC-B includes Hindu OBC communities
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • Madhyamik / School Certificate showing caste
  • Land Records / Record of Rights (RoR)
  • Old Ration Card with caste entry
Additional (OBC)
  • Income Certificate
  • Permanent Residence Certificate
  • Self-declaration affidavit
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-District West Bengal

Apply at e-District West Bengal Portal

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to edistrict.wb.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Register with your mobile number and email. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select Service
    Navigate to Revenue Department → SC/ST Certificate or Backward Classes Welfare → OBC Certificate.
  4. Fill Application
    Enter name, DOB, caste, father's name, native district, block, and GP/municipality details.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of all required documents (PDF/JPG).
  6. Submit & Track
    Submit and track status using your application reference number.
  7. Download Certificate
    Download the digitally signed certificate from the portal once approved.
You can also apply via Duare Sarkar camps organised periodically by the West Bengal government.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at BDO / SDO Office

  1. Visit BDO or SDO Office
    Go to the Block Development Officer (BDO) or Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) office of your native block/subdivision.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Caste Certificate application form and fill all details.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit self-attested document copies with the filled form. Get acknowledgement receipt.
  4. Field Verification
    A Gram Panchayat officer or Village Level Worker (VLW) may conduct a field inquiry for verification.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect your caste certificate from the BDO / SDO office within 15–30 working days.
Processing Time: 15–30 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at e-District Portal using your application reference number.

Duare Sarkar Camps

Keep an eye on Duare Sarkar camp schedules in your district — you can apply and collect certificates conveniently at these camps.

Grievance

Call 1800-345-5555 or visit WB e-Governance Portal for complaints.
